name: Twist Async Team Communication API
what_it_does: Asynchronous team communication platform with REST API — enabling remote and distributed teams to communicate in organized threads (not real-time chat), with API access for channel and thread management, message posting, integration building, and automation, built by the Doist team (creators of Todoist) with a strong emphasis on async-first communication culture.
use_cases: Sending automated thread messages via Twist API for workflow notifications in async format
Building integrations via Twist API for cross-tool updates to team threads
Managing channels and threads via Twist API for team communication automation
Posting structured updates via Twist API for async-friendly status reporting
Integrating CI/CD events via Twist for development team async notifications
not_for: Real-time chat use cases (Twist is intentionally async — no real-time channel presence)
Large enterprise with complex identity/compliance requirements (Twist is SMB/startup-focused)
Teams that need rich Slack app ecosystem integrations
best_when: A remote-first or distributed team prioritizes async communication over real-time chat — where Twist's threaded format and API enable structured communication without the noise and interruptions of Slack.
avoid_when: Your team needs real-time presence awareness, rapid-fire chat, or extensive third-party app integrations.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow for app integrations. Personal API tokens for personal automation (no OAuth). Bearer token authentication. Scoped OAuth2 (read/write per resource type). Webhooks for thread and comment events. Incoming webhooks for posting to channels. Bot users with oauth token.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: unlimited messages, 1 month history, 5GB storage
pricing.notes: Created by Doist (also makes Todoist). Doist is a remote-first company, Twist is their internal async communication tool made public. Portuguese company (Porto). Bootstrapped and profitable. 100,000+ users. Remote work philosophy deeply embedded. Threads (not channels) as primary organization. Strong async culture content marketing. Used by: remote teams, agencies, distributed startups. Less known than Slack but strong loyal user base. API v3 is current. Integration-friendly. Developer documentation is good. GitHub Actions integration. Zapier integration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v3 with OAuth2 and personal tokens. Webhooks. GDPR compliant. Portuguese/EU company. Free plan. Async-first design. Good developer documentation. Thread-based (not channel-based) data model.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Thread reads idempotent by thread ID. Comment reads idempotent. Message posting not idempotent — check for existing messages. Channel reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Thread-based model: messages exist within threads, not directly in channels — create thread first, then add comments
Personal API token vs OAuth app token — different use cases: personal=automation, OAuth=integrations
Async philosophy means no read receipts or presence — don't design for real-time patterns
Portuguese company — GDPR by default; EU data residency
Free plan: 1-month message history — paid required for full history access via API
Rate limits not documented — implement conservative rate limiting in integrations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Portuguese company (EU). OAuth2 with resource scopes. Personal token and OAuth token options. EU data residency. Bootstrapped company — privacy-conscious culture. Chat data (team communication) is sensitive internal communication.
